summary: See clues file for full details. 3 tossups, 5 bonuses found.

works.name: General / Key Works
works.description: Analysis from 3 tossups and 5 bonuses. See clues file for detailed breakdown.

clues.clue: These images are generally said to have been invented by a 16th-century painter from Bologna who said that the task of creating these images was exactly the same as the task of the classical artist.
examples: These images are generally said to have been invented by a 16th-century painter from Bologna who said that the task of creating these images was exactly the same as the task of the classical artist.

clues.clue: One of these images depicts a bunch of people at a table snoring as Samuel Taylor Coleridge talks, and appears in the collection The Poets' Corner.
examples: One of these images depicts a bunch of people at a table snoring as Samuel Taylor Coleridge talks, and appears in the collection The Poets' Corner.

clues.clue: More than a thousand of these images, many of them drawn by Alex Gard, adorn the walls of (*) Sardi's restaurant in Manhattan.
examples: More than a thousand of these images, many of them drawn by Alex Gard, adorn the walls of (*) Sardi's restaurant in Manhattan.

clues.clue: Aubrey Beardsley is one of the "twenty-five gentlemen" depicted in a book that established Max Beerbohm's reputation as a creator of these images.
examples: Aubrey Beardsley is one of the "twenty-five gentlemen" depicted in a book that established Max Beerbohm's reputation as a creator of these images.

clues.clue: In a satirical French magazine named after these images, Louis-Philippe was often depicted as a pear.
examples: In a satirical French magazine named after these images, Louis-Philippe was often depicted as a pear.

clues.clue: This artist prepended the letter "f" to his name in the only signature in his oeuvre, which is visible in a pool of blood in a painting that was executed as a passage fee for the Knights of Malta.
examples: This artist prepended the letter "f" to his name in the only signature in his oeuvre, which is visible in a pool of blood in a painting that was executed as a passage fee for the Knights of Malta.

clues.clue: This artist had to repaint one work to show a bearded man kneeling on a stool in response to complaints that he'd placed a dirty foot too close to the altar.
examples: This artist had to repaint one work to show a bearded man kneeling on a stool in response to complaints that he'd placed a dirty foot too close to the altar.

clues.clue: This artist mocked a rival by depicting a horse's buttocks facing toward that rival's work in his painting for the Cerasi  Chapel.
examples: This artist mocked a rival by depicting a horse's buttocks facing toward that rival's work in his painting for the Cerasi  Chapel.

clues.clue: This artist of The (*) Beheading of Saint John the Baptist fled Rome after killing a man in a street fight.
examples: This artist of The (*) Beheading of Saint John the Baptist fled Rome after killing a man in a street fight.

clues.clue: He depicted a saint's "inspiration" in one of his works for the Contarelli Chapel, another of which depicts a group of men huddled around a table as a bright light illuminates a man being chosen as Jesus's disciple.
examples: He depicted a saint's "inspiration" in one of his works for the Contarelli Chapel, another of which depicts a group of men huddled around a table as a bright light illuminates a man being chosen as Jesus's disciple.

clues.clue: One of this man's paintings includes a depiction of Luxuria, or Vice, with her back to the viewer, arranged in a pose that was borrowed by Caravaggio for the violin-playing angel in a painting created for the Palazzo Doria Pamphilj.
examples: One of this man's paintings includes a depiction of Luxuria, or Vice, with her back to the viewer, arranged in a pose that was borrowed by Caravaggio for the violin-playing angel in a painting created for the Palazzo Doria Pamphilj.

clues.clue: This artist painted The Choice of Hercules for the camerino of a building that also contains a series by this man consisting of an early example of quadri riportati depicting scenes from Ovid.
examples: This artist painted The Choice of Hercules for the camerino of a building that also contains a series by this man consisting of an early example of quadri riportati depicting scenes from Ovid.
